text.value: The html source contains the word 'SHALL' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: 'All risk adjustment coding gap reports contained in a Risk Adjustment Coding Gap Report Bundle SHALL be for the same patient.'
The html source contains the word 'SHALL' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: 'evaluatedResource SHALL reference resources using USCore profiles where applicable'

The html source contains the word 'SHALL' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: 'One or more Group.member element(s) and each member SHALL reference a US Core Patient resource'

The html source contains the word 'SHALL NOT' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: 'The Provider's system being used SHALL NOT change any other part of the original Risk Adjustment Coding Gap Report and can only add the Condition Category Remark to the appropriate MeasureReport.group(s). If a provider wants to share data with the Payer in order to change a coding gap status, they should use the Submit Data to Payer process.'

The html source contains the word 'SHALL' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: 'Certain elements in the profiles defined in this implementation guide are marked as Must Support. This flag is used to indicate that the element plays a critical role in defining and sharing risk adjustment coding gaps, and implementations SHALL understand and process the element.'

The html source contains the word 'SHALL' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: 'Note that both Prefer and Accept are required. Prefer specifies the response is immediate or asynchronous, which SHALL be set to respond-async. Accept specifies the format of the optional OperationOutcome response to the kick-off request. Any of the Serialization Format Representations are supported. See the base FHIR specification Asynchronous Request Patterns for details.'

The html source contains the word 'SHALL' but it is not in a text phrase marked as a conformance clause: 'This implementation guide inherits all of the mandatory requirements and recommendations defined in the Security and Privacy section of the Da Vinci Health Record Exchange (HRex) implementaion guide. Implementers SHALL read and adhere to the guidance for the following topics: Statutes, Regulations FHIR Clinical Safy, Security and Implementation Guidance Security/Privacy Related Technologies, Including Explicit Consent and Security Labels Exchange Security Additionally Protected Information Security Contexts for Da Vinci IGs '
The HTML fragment 'expansion-params.xhtml' is not included anywhere in the produced implementation guide
Pinned the version of http://hl7.org/fhir/us/core/StructureDefinition/us-core-condition-encounter-diagnosis to 7.0.0 from choices of 6.1.0 (hl7.fhir.us.core#6.1.0), 7.0.0 (hl7.fhir.us.core#7.0.0)

No definition could be found for URL value 'http://hl7.org/fhir/tools/StructureDefinition/expansion-parameters'

The ImplementationGuide uses package hl7.fhir.us.core#7.0.0 released on 2024-05-08, but the most recent appropriate version is 8.0.1. This reference is getting old and the more recent version should be considered
The concepts in the value set include have not been validated because the code system is not known, and no terminology service supports it
Error from https://tx.fhir.org/r4: Error: A definition for CodeSystem 'http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/cmshcc' could not be found, so the value set cannot be expanded
